verses.latin_vulgate: Verti me ad alia, et vidi calumnias  quæ sub sole geruntur,  et lacrimas innocentium,  et neminem consolatorem,  nec posse resistere eorum violentiæ,  cunctorum auxilio destitutos,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשַׁ֣בְתִּֽי אֲנִ֗י וָאֶרְאֶה֙ אֶת־כׇּל־הָ֣עֲשֻׁקִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר נַעֲשִׂ֖ים תַּ֣חַת הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ וְהִנֵּ֣ה ׀ דִּמְעַ֣ת הָעֲשֻׁקִ֗ים וְאֵ֤ין לָהֶם֙ מְנַחֵ֔ם וּמִיַּ֤ד עֹֽשְׁקֵיהֶם֙ כֹּ֔חַ וְאֵ֥ין לָהֶ֖ם מְנַחֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: So I returned, and considered all the oppressions that are done under the sun: and behold the tears of such as were oppressed, and they had no comforter; and on the side of their oppressors there was power; but they had no comforter.

verses.latin_vulgate: et laudavi magis mortuos quam viventes ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשַׁבֵּ֧חַ אֲנִ֛י אֶת־הַמֵּתִ֖ים שֶׁכְּבָ֣ר מֵ֑תוּ מִן־הַ֣חַיִּ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֛ר הֵ֥מָּה חַיִּ֖ים עֲדֶֽנָה׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore I praised the dead which are already dead more than the living which are yet alive.

verses.latin_vulgate: et feliciorem utroque judicavi  qui necdum natus est,  nec vidit mala quæ sub sole fiunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְטוֹב֙ מִשְּׁנֵיהֶ֔ם אֵ֥ת אֲשֶׁר־עֲדֶ֖ן לֹ֣א הָיָ֑ה אֲשֶׁ֤ר לֹֽא־רָאָה֙ אֶת־הַמַּעֲשֶׂ֣ה הָרָ֔ע אֲשֶׁ֥ר נַעֲשָׂ֖ה תַּ֥חַת הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Yea, better is he than both they, which hath not yet been, who hath not seen the evil work that is done under the sun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rursum contemplatus sum omnes labores hominum,  et industrias animadverti patere invidiæ proximi ;  et in hoc ergo vanitas et cura superflua est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְרָאִ֨יתִֽי אֲנִ֜י אֶת־כׇּל־עָמָ֗ל וְאֵת֙ כׇּל־כִּשְׁר֣וֹן הַֽמַּעֲשֶׂ֔ה כִּ֛י הִ֥יא קִנְאַת־אִ֖ישׁ מֵרֵעֵ֑הוּ גַּם־זֶ֥ה הֶ֖בֶל וּרְע֥וּת רֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: Again, I considered all travail, and every right work, that for this a man is envied of his neighbour. This is also vanity and vexation of spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultus complicat manus suas,  et comedit carnes suas, dicens :
verses.king_james: The fool foldeth his hands together, and eateth his own flesh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est pugillus cum requie,  quam plena utraque manus cum labore et afflictione animi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֕וֹב מְלֹ֥א כַ֖ף נָ֑חַת מִמְּלֹ֥א חׇפְנַ֛יִם עָמָ֖ל וּרְע֥וּת רֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: Better is an handful with quietness, than both the hands full with travail and vexation of spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Considerans, reperi et aliam vanitatem sub sole.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשַׁ֧בְתִּי אֲנִ֛י וָאֶרְאֶ֥ה הֶ֖בֶל תַּ֥חַת הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Then I returned, and I saw vanity under the sun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unus est, et secundum non habet,  non filium, non fratrem,  et tamen laborare non cessat,  nec satiantur oculi ejus divitiis ;  nec recogitat, dicens :  Cui laboro, et fraudo animam meam bonis ?  In hoc quoque vanitas est et afflictio pessima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֣שׁ אֶחָד֩ וְאֵ֨ין שֵׁנִ֜י גַּ֣ם בֵּ֧ן וָאָ֣ח אֵֽין־ל֗וֹ וְאֵ֥ין קֵץ֙ לְכׇל־עֲמָל֔וֹ גַּם־[עֵינ֖וֹ] (עיניו) לֹא־תִשְׂבַּ֣ע עֹ֑שֶׁר וּלְמִ֣י ׀ אֲנִ֣י עָמֵ֗ל וּמְחַסֵּ֤ר אֶת־נַפְשִׁי֙ מִטּוֹבָ֔ה גַּם־זֶ֥ה הֶ֛בֶל וְעִנְיַ֥ן רָ֖ע הֽוּא׃
verses.king_james: There is one alone, and there is not a second; yea, he hath neither child nor brother: yet is there no end of all his labour; neither is his eye satisfied with riches; neither saith he, For whom do I labour, and bereave my soul of good? This is also vanity, yea, it is a sore travail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est ergo duos esse simul quam unum ;  habent enim emolumentum societatis suæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טוֹבִ֥ים הַשְּׁנַ֖יִם מִן־הָאֶחָ֑ד אֲשֶׁ֧ר יֵשׁ־לָהֶ֛ם שָׂכָ֥ר ט֖וֹב בַּעֲמָלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Two are better than one; because they have a good reward for their labour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si unus ceciderit, ab altero fulcietur.  Væ soli, quia cum ceciderit, non habet sublevantem se.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י אִם־יִפֹּ֔לוּ הָאֶחָ֖ד יָקִ֣ים אֶת־חֲבֵר֑וֹ וְאִ֣יל֗וֹ הָֽאֶחָד֙ שֶׁיִּפּ֔וֹל וְאֵ֥ין שֵׁנִ֖י לַהֲקִימֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow: but woe to him that is alone when he falleth; for he hath not another to help him up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si dormierint duo, fovebuntur mutuo ;  unus quomodo calefiet ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֛ם אִם־יִשְׁכְּב֥וּ שְׁנַ֖יִם וְחַ֣ם לָהֶ֑ם וּלְאֶחָ֖ד אֵ֥יךְ יֵחָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Again, if two lie together, then they have heat: but how can one be warm alone?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et si quispiam prævaluerit contra unum,  duo resistunt ei ;  funiculus triplex difficile rumpitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִֽם־יִתְקְפוֹ֙ הָאֶחָ֔ד הַשְּׁנַ֖יִם יַעַמְד֣וּ נֶגְדּ֑וֹ וְהַחוּט֙ הַֽמְשֻׁלָּ֔שׁ לֹ֥א בִמְהֵרָ֖ה יִנָּתֵֽק׃
verses.king_james: And if one prevail against him, two shall withstand him; and a threefold cord is not quickly broken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melior est puer pauper et sapiens,  rege sene et stulto,  qui nescit prævidere in posterum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֛וֹב יֶ֥לֶד מִסְכֵּ֖ן וְחָכָ֑ם מִמֶּ֤לֶךְ זָקֵן֙ וּכְסִ֔יל אֲשֶׁ֛ר לֹא־יָדַ֥ע לְהִזָּהֵ֖ר עֽוֹד׃
verses.king_james: Better is a poor and a wise child than an old and foolish king, who will no more be admonished.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod de carcere catenisque interdum quis egrediatur ad regnum ;  et alius, natus in regno, inopia consumatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־מִבֵּ֥ית הָסוּרִ֖ים יָצָ֣א לִמְלֹ֑ךְ כִּ֛י גַּ֥ם בְּמַלְכוּת֖וֹ נוֹלַ֥ד רָֽשׁ׃
verses.king_james: For out of prison he cometh to reign; whereas also he that is born in his kingdom becometh poor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidi cunctos viventes qui ambulant sub sole  cum adolescente secundo, qui consurget pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָאִ֙יתִי֙ אֶת־כׇּל־הַ֣חַיִּ֔ים הַֽמְהַלְּכִ֖ים תַּ֣חַת הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ עִ֚ם הַיֶּ֣לֶד הַשֵּׁנִ֔י אֲשֶׁ֥ר יַעֲמֹ֖ד תַּחְתָּֽיו׃
verses.king_james: I considered all the living which walk under the sun, with the second child that shall stand up in his stead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Infinitus numerus est populi  omnium qui fuerunt ante eum,  et qui postea futuri sunt non lætabuntur in eo ;  sed et hoc vanitas et afflictio spiritus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵֽין־קֵ֣ץ לְכׇל־הָעָ֗ם לְכֹ֤ל אֲשֶׁר־הָיָה֙ לִפְנֵיהֶ֔ם גַּ֥ם הָאַחֲרוֹנִ֖ים לֹ֣א יִשְׂמְחוּ־ב֑וֹ כִּֽי־גַם־זֶ֥ה הֶ֖בֶל וְרַעְי֥וֹן רֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: There is no end of all the people, even of all that have been before them: they also that come after shall not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and vexation of spirit.
